profile.name: Ankit Singh
profile.bio:
𝘍𝘶𝘭𝘭-𝘚𝘵𝘢𝘤𝘬 𝘌𝘯𝘨𝘪𝘯𝘦𝘦𝘳 • 𝘈𝘐/𝘔𝘓 𝘚𝘺𝘴𝘵𝘦𝘮𝘴  
𝘉𝘶𝘪𝘭𝘥𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘴𝘤𝘢𝘭𝘢𝘣𝘭𝘦, 𝘱𝘳𝘰𝘥𝘶𝘤𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯-𝘨𝘳𝘢𝘥𝘦 𝘱𝘭𝘢𝘵𝘧𝘰𝘳𝘮𝘴

prs.title: refactor: use strict equality in RegExp exec checks
prs.body: This PR refactors `!= null` to `!== null` for `RegExp.exec` return value checks in `src/Lexer.ts` and `src/Tokenizer.ts`.\nSince `RegExp.exec` only returns `RegExpExecArray` or `null`, `!==` perfectly aligns with the required behavior and improves strict equality consistency across the codebase.

prs.title: refactor: modernize utils and xhr adapter using ES6 features
prs.body:
This PR modernizes several parts of the codebase by:
1. Replacing `arguments` with rest parameters (`...objs`) in the `merge` function.
2. Replacing `indexOf(...) !== -1` with `.includes(...)` for better readability.
3. Replacing `indexOf(...) === 0` with `.startsWith(...)` in the XHR adapter.

These changes align with modern JavaScript standards already present in the Axios 1.x codebase.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Modernizes utils and the XHR adapter to ES6 (rest params, .includes, .startsWith) for clarity and consistency with `v1.x`. Synced with the latest `v1.x`; no behavior or API changes.

**Description**
- Switched `merge` to rest params (keeps `caseless`/`skipUndefined`).
- Replaced `indexOf` with `.includes(...)` across utils/XHR, including the restricted props check in `freezeMethods`.
- Used `.startsWith('file:')` for `file:` URL detection; protocol allowlist now uses `.includes(...)`.
- Docs: no user-facing changes; consider updating `/docs/` coding standards to prefer ES6 patterns.
- Semantic version impact: patch (refactor only).

**Testing**
- No tests changed; existing tests for XHR protocol handling and `utils.merge` are sufficient.

<sup>Written for commit 02dd11704dc53d6093672db3cf67a3b4304cc2eb. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

prs.title: fix: use strict equality for type check in unlinkSync
prs.body: Changed loose equality (==) to strict equality (===) for checking the type of filepath in unlinkSync utility. This is a minor code quality improvement to avoid type coercion.

prs.title: fix: use strict equality in redo move action
prs.body: Changed loose equality (==) to strict equality (===) for table ID comparison in the redo action for moving tables. This ensures consistent type comparison throughout the component.

prs.title: refactor: simplify shortcuts property with Object.fromEntries
prs.body: Replaced `reduce` with `Object.fromEntries` for the shortcuts property definition. This makes the code more readable and allows removing the `eslint-disable` comment for `unicorn/no-array-reduce`.

prs.title: feat: improve accessibility for course progress bars
prs.body: Added role="progressbar", aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax to the course progress bars to improve accessibility for screen readers.

prs.title: feat: add ARIA accessibility attributes to ProgressBar
prs.body: Implemented `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax` as requested in the component's TODO. This improves accessibility for screen readers by providing progress information.

prs.title: refactor: replace deprecated .substr() with .substring()
prs.body: String.prototype.substr() is deprecated per MDN. This PR replaces all occurrences in the test file with the standard .substring() method.

prs.title: chore: remove debug console.log statements
prs.body:
Removed leftover debug `console.log` statements from `copy-file-server.ts` and `move-dataroom-documents.ts` to keep production logs clean.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Removed debug logging statements from document and file operations to improve code cleanliness and reduce unnecessary console output.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix: correct 'occured' typo in error messages
prs.body: Fixed the misspelling of 'occurred' (was 'occured') in two user-facing error strings in auth.js and QrScanner.jsx.

prs.title: fix: use strict equality in ImportDiagram
prs.body: Changed `==` to `===` when checking `importFrom` type to ensure strict equality comparisons and resolve potential type coercion issues.

prs.title: fix: use strict equality in type check
